The [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn]] '''7-PrG Proton Grenade''', or '''Sequencer Charge''', was a powerful explosive developed for structure demolition.

==Description==

The 7-PrG proton grenade was manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]] and were designed to breach [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]] during boarding actions. They could also be used for large-scale demolition projects. 7-PrG [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] were cylindrical devices with a flared base.<ref name="EGWT" />

The base of the 7-PrG contained a [[magnetic plate]] that allowed the grenade to be affixed to a surface. The powerful [[proton core]] was held in the main cylinder of the grenade, which was primed by twisting the arming mechanism at the base of the explosive. Pressing the activation plunger started the grenade's [[timer]]; the 7-PrG utilized a dial timer that allowed a maximum of twenty [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] to elapse before the grenade exploded. Disarming the device required pressing the twist plunger release at the top of the grenade.<ref name="EGWT" />

The power of the 7-PrG's proton core was comparable to a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]], so it was important that the grenade had a two-stage activation sequence to prevent accidental detonation.<ref name="EGWT" />

==History==
The 7-PrG was used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to destroy the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield bunker]] there.<ref name="EGWT" /> This brought down the protective shield over the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]], allowing Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to penetrate its interior and destroy its power generator during the climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="SWE6">{{Film|VI}}</ref> They were placed in the control room and in the generator room.<ref name="RS III">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
7-PrG proton grenades first appeared in the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].
